source_pr.title: planner, executor: support access path `keep order` with `IN` conditions using merge sort
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #63449 close #34882

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

- planner
  - As mentioned in the issue, new fields `GroupedRanges` and `GroupByColIdxs` are added to `AccessPath`, `PhysicalIndexScan`, and `PhysicalTableScan`. (`pkg/planner/core/operator/physicalop/*`)
  - `matchProperty()` (`pkg/planner/core/find_best_task.go`)
    - Add the new logic to match the sort property as described in the issue.
  - Due to changes in `matchProperty()`, the names and data types of several other fields and the function return value are also modified correspondingly, like `candidatePath.isMatchProp`, `matchPropForIndexMergeAlternatives()`. The checking logic is also updated correspondingly. (`pkg/planner/core/find_best_task.go`)
  - When it's `PropMatchedNeedMergeSort`, set up `ByItems`, `GroupedRanges`, and `GroupByColIdxs` for `PhysicalIndexScan` and `PhysicalTableScan`. (`convertToIndexScan()`, `convertToTableScan()`)
  - New function `GroupRangesByCols()` that builds/rebuilds the `GroupedRanges` from the `GroupByColIdxs` and `ranges`. (`pkg/planner/core/find_best_task.go`)
  - Enum `PhysicalPropMatchResult` (`pkg/planner/property/physical_property.go`)
    - `PropMatchedNeedMergeSort`: means the newly introduced enhancement.
  - When rebuilding ranges for the plan cache, also rebuild the `GroupedRanges`. (`pkg/planner/core/plan_cache_rebuild.go`)
- executor
  - In `Open()` of the 3 executors, when there are correlated columns, also use `GroupRangesByCols` to rebuild grouped ranges after rebuilding ranges.
  - `IndexReaderExecutor`
    - New field `groupedRanges [][]*ranger.Range`, directly comes from `PhysicalIndexScan`.
    - Update `Open()` to consider the new case when building kv ranges.
  - `IndexLookUpExecutor`
    - New field `groupedRanges [][]*ranger.Range`, directly comes from `PhysicalIndexScan`.
    - New field `groupedKVRanges []*kvRangesWithPhysicalTblID` as a unified place to store the kv ranges. It replaces the previous `partitionKVRanges` and `kvRanges` field.
    - Update `buildTableKeyRanges()` to consider the new case when building kv ranges.
  - `TableReaderExecutor`
    - New field `groupedRanges` and `groupByColIdxs`, directly come from `PhysicalTableScan`.
    - Update `Open()` to consider the new case when building kv ranges.
    - `buildResp()` is updated and renamed to `buildRespForGroupedRanges()` as a unified method to handle ranges and grouped ranges.
  - `mem*Reader`
    - Correctly set up `usedIndex` for the extra sorting (mem reader doesn't support merge sort). (`pkg/executor/mem_reader.go`)
    - When `usedIndex` is set, it means we need extra sorting. (`pkg/executor/mem_reader.go`)
    - `memIndexLookUpReader`
      - New field `groupedKVRanges []*kvRangesWithPhysicalTblID` as a unified place to store the kv ranges. It replaces the previous `partitionKVRanges`, `partitionTables`, and `partitionMode` fields. It corresponds to the changes in `IndexLookUpExecutor`.


### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: explain format = 'brief' select /*+ order_index(t2,iabc) */ a,c from t2 where a = 3 and b in (1, 3, 5) order by a desc, c desc; (tests/integrationtest/r/planner/core/grouped_ranges_order_by.result)
failure_signature: explain format = 'brief' select /*+ order_index(t2,iabc) */ a,c from t2 where a = 3 and b in (1, 3, 5) order by a desc, c desc; (tests/integrationtest/r/planner/core/grouped_ranges_order_by.result)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted an exact plan/EXPLAIN output that can vary between runs. Exact plan/EXPLAIN assertions are brittle when optimizer choices can vary. This PR stabilizes it by: planner As mentioned in the issue, new fields `GroupedRanges` and `GroupByColIdxs` are added to `AccessPath`, `PhysicalIndexScan`, and `PhysicalTableScan`.
fix_pattern: planner As mentioned in the issue, new fields `GroupedRanges` and `GroupByColIdxs` are added to `AccessPath`, `PhysicalIndexScan`, and `PhysicalTableScan`. (`pkg/planner/core/operator/physicalop/*`) `matchProperty()` (`pkg/planner/core/find_best_task.go`) Add the new logic to match the sort property as described in the issue. Due to changes in `matchProperty()`, the names and data types of several other fields and the function return value are also modified correspondingly, like `candidatePath.isMatchProp`, `matchPropForIndexMergeAlternatives()`. The checking logic is also updated correspondingly. (`pkg/planner/core/find_best_task.go`) When it's `PropMatchedNeedMergeSort`, set up `ByItems`, `GroupedRanges`, and `GroupByColIdxs` for `PhysicalIndexScan` and `PhysicalTableScan`. (`convertToIndexScan()`, `convertToTableScan()`) New function `GroupRangesByCols()` that builds/rebuilds the `GroupedRanges` from the `GroupByColIdxs` and `ranges`. (`pkg/planner/core/find_best_task.go`) Enum `PhysicalPropMatchResult` (`pkg/planner/property/physical_property.go`) `PropMatchedNeedMergeSort`: means the newly introduced enhancement. When rebuilding ranges for the plan cache, also rebuild the `GroupedRanges`. (`pkg/planner/core/plan_cache_rebuild.go`) executor In `Open()` of the 3 executors, when there are correlated columns, also use `GroupRangesByCols` to rebuild grouped ranges after rebuilding ranges. `IndexReaderExecutor` New field `groupedRanges [][]*ranger.Range`, directly comes from `PhysicalIndexScan`. Update `Open()` to consider the new case when building kv ranges. `IndexLookUpExecutor` New field `groupedRanges [][]*ranger.Range`, directly comes from `PhysicalIndexScan`. New field `groupedKVRanges []*kvRangesWithPhysicalTblID` as a unified place to store the kv ranges. It replaces the previous `partitionKVRanges` and `kvRanges` field. Update `buildTableKeyRanges()` to consider the new case when building kv ranges. `TableReaderExecutor` New field `groupedRanges` and `groupByColIdxs`, directly come from `PhysicalTableScan`. Update `Open()` to consider the new case when building kv ranges. `buildResp()` is updated and renamed to `buildRespForGroupedRanges()` as a unified method to handle ranges and grouped ranges. `mem*Reader` Correctly set up `usedIndex` for the extra sorting (mem reader doesn't support merge sort). (`pkg/executor/mem_reader.go`) When `usedIndex` is set, it means we need extra sorting. (`pkg/executor/mem_reader.go`) `memIndexLookUpReader` New field `groupedKVRanges []*kvRangesWithPhysicalTblID` as a unified place to store the kv ranges. It replaces the previous `partitionKVRanges`, `partitionTables`, and `partitionMode` fields. It corresponds to the changes in `IndexLookUpExecutor`.
